description: run NV-Segment-CTMR VISTA3D inference on a CT/MRI NIfTI volume and record label-map evidence
rules: thin wrapper — inference/preprocessing/postprocessing delegated entirely to the upstream MONAI bundle; do not reimplement
shells out to the official entrypoint 'python -m monai.bundle run --config_file configs/inference.json' only
requires the upstream checkout at $NV_SEGMENT_CTMR_ROOT (or the repo-local .workbench_data fallback) plus downloaded model.pt weights
modality must be one of CT_BODY | MRI_BODY | MRI_BRAIN; MRI_BRAIN input must already be brain-preprocessed
writes mask outputs + result JSON under record_store; no clinical, diagnostic, or regulatory claims
usage: Set CT_OR_MR_VOLUME (absolute path to the .nii/.nii.gz input) and MODALITY (CT_BODY default). Output: result.json plus the produced NIfTI label map under record_store.
limitation: Thin wrapper; all inference is delegated to the upstream MONAI bundle under $NV_SEGMENT_CTMR_ROOT or the repo-local fallback. Default path runs automatic 'segment everything' for CT_BODY/MRI_BODY/MRI_BRAIN; MRI_BRAIN must already meet upstream brain preprocessing requirements. Label names come from upstream configs when present; absent a label dict only negative IDs are marked invalid. No clinical, diagnostic, regulatory, or treatment-planning claims; not for clinical deployment, interpretation, autonomous diagnosis, or regulatory submission.
